/pride.summary: A personal-development essay using the Pulp Fiction "pride is fucking with you" scene as a frame to argue that pride, arrogance, and hubris corrupt good decision-making. Igor catalogs personal examples (comparing his career to a peer's, feeling treated unfairly, needing to be right) with antidotes for each, ultimately clarifying the post is really about arrogance and hubris rather than pride.

/process-journal.title: Daily Journaling: From Psychic Diarrhea to Polished Turds
/process-journal.summary: Igor's aspirational daily journaling workflow: handwriting on a Kindle Scribe, then AI transcription and automation to convert it to searchable text, extract TODOs to OmniFocus, and process entries against a reflection template of gratitude, affirmations, and psychic weight.

/produce-consume.title: The Producer's Paradox: Why Creating Beats Consuming Every Time
/produce-consume.summary: An argument that producing things — making artifacts that deliver value and build compounding skills — beats passive consumption of algorithmic content, with a warning that production itself can become a dopamine-driven addiction when it crowds out presence and relationships.

/product.title: Building products
/product.summary: A product management reference and field-notes post covering the full arc of product work—vision, strategy, discovery, design, and prioritization—plus deeper concepts like stated vs. revealed preferences, desire paths, gamification, business/funding models, and revenue models.

/productive.title: Productive
/productive.summary: An outline-style notes post on the fundamentals of personal productivity, framing energy as the fuel of productivity and covering matching tasks to energy levels, habits, momentum, and flow, alongside the obstacles (resistance, anxiety, addiction, procrastination).

/prompts.title: Prompts, your guide to starting a conversation
/prompts.summary: A curated collection of conversation prompts organized by context—work 1:1s, friendship-deepening exercises, and questions for couples and families—designed to spark trust, vulnerability, and meaningful connection. The prompts range from honest-feedback and career-growth questions to Berkeley's closeness-building exercises and lighthearted relationship icebreakers.

/psychic-shadows.title: Psychic Shadows: Crafting Spells to Banish Ruminating Thoughts
/psychic-shadows.summary: A method for handling recurring ruminating thoughts called psychic shadows by analyzing each one and distilling it into a single spell word that interrupts the mental loop and redirects attention; it separates functional pain that needs action from mental suffering that needs banishment and catalogs financial, health, relationship, and identity shadow types.

/psychic-weight.title: Psychic Weight
/psychic-weight.summary: A personal taxonomy of "psychic weight" — the recurring thoughts and unprocessed emotions (aversion, rumination, shame, pride, grasping, triggers) that drag on the mind — paired with antidotes like meditation, Getting Things Done, separating pain from suffering, and the 7 Habits' Circle of Influence. Igor grounds it with examples from his own life.

/quip.title: Quip tips and tricks
/quip.summary: A short collection of personal tips and FAQ for using Quip, the shared document editor, covering interface settings, keyboard shortcuts, and comparisons to alternatives.

/raccoon-history.title: The Raccoon Mascot: A Visual History
/raccoon-history.summary: A history of the blog's anthropomorphic raccoon mascot, tracing its evolution from inconsistent 2022 DALL-E experiments through a 2025 gpt-image-1 brand refresh to a codified 2026 brand system, plus the workflows (reference images, choice sheets, background removal) that made consistent AI character generation reliable.

/random-idea.title: Random Uncategorized Notes
/random-idea.summary: A personal scratchpad collecting Igor's rough, uncategorized ideas — spanning self-improvement, business/personal branding, mental models, journaling prompts, relationship-building, and curated wisdom drawn from books and lectures.
entities: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People

/recent.title: Recently Modified Content
/recent.summary: A utility index page that uses client-side JavaScript to fetch site metadata from back-links.json and display recently modified posts in reverse chronological order, plus a random-post discovery widget. It explains why tracking recent changes aids knowledge management and how the page works technically.

/recommend.title: Recommender Systems
/recommend.summary: Igor's working study notes on recommender and ranking systems, based on the book Practical Recommender Systems. It walks through candidate generation, scoring, and re-ranking; implicit vs explicit ratings; collaborative and content-based filtering; similarity metrics; cold-start problems; and how to test and evaluate recommenders.

/regrets.title: Use Your Regrets As A Compass, Not A Cage
/regrets.summary: A reframing of regret as a forward-looking compass rather than a backward-looking cage, drawing on Daniel Pink's research to categorize regrets into four types (foundation, boldness, moral, connection) and pairing them with practices like self-compassion, circle-of-influence thinking, and leading with curiosity over judgment.
entities: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People

/religion.title: Religion: Losing belief while seeing value
/religion.summary: A personal essay where Igor, an engineer who demands proof, reckons with the value of religion — not through metaphysics but through the structure, ritual, and community it provided when his father died — and proposes that "God" secretly performs ten distinct roles (love, confidence, moral framework, community, etc.) that secular people must cobble together individually.

/remote-work.title: Remote Work
/remote-work.summary: Igor's reflections as a remote engineering manager at Facebook during the Covid era, covering remote-meeting practices, sync-vs-async communication, building team culture and rapport at a distance, and the shift from email to chat and collaborative-document tooling.

/retire.title: Retirement: Stop Planning the Exit, Start Planning the Life
/retire.summary: A holistic reframing of retirement that argues the real goal isn't escaping work but building a life of autonomy, mastery, and purpose (AMP) — and that you should start cultivating hobbies, identity, health, and meaning while still employed rather than waiting. Covers financial independence vs. retirement, FIRE variants, health as a non-renewable asset, and common retirement myths.

/roblox-dev.title: Developing on roblox
/roblox-dev.summary: A working-notes post on getting started with Roblox game development alongside the author's kids, focused on the Lua scripting toolchain — VS Code plugins, linters, language servers, and package managers.

/save-the-soup.title: Save the Soup
/save-the-soup.summary: A humorous personal story from Igor's college days: he and his roommate Biff move to Seattle for a Microsoft internship, get drunk on scotch while cooking soup, end up calling 911 over the roommate's alcohol-induced misery, and the next morning the roommate's only concern is whether the soup was saved.

/save-the-souse.title: Save the Souse
/save-the-souse.summary: A humorous personal anecdote from Igor's Microsoft internship about stopping his drunk friend Biff from driving by tricking him into a balcony jump challenge, framed around the buddy system and the obligation to keep friends safe. Lands on two morals: never drive drunk, and protect your friends by any creative means necessary.

/screencast.title: Screencasting on OSX
/screencast.summary: A practical guide to Igor's screencasting workflow on macOS, covering screen and facecam recording, editing, captioning, and hosting, plus a rundown of tools he tried and ultimately cut from his pipeline.

/seattle.title: Seattle
/seattle.summary: A curated personal guide to Seattle, listing Igor's favorite coffee shops, neighborhoods, hikes, restaurants, beaches, parks, and tourist attractions for visitors to the city.

/self-ego.title: The Self vs Ego: Jung's Map for Living Authentically
/self-ego.summary: A personal essay applying Carl Jung's Ego-Self framework (via Edward Edinger) to the author's own life, distinguishing the social, achievement-driven Ego from the authentic, whole Self. It maps a four-stage cycle (inflation, deflation, dialogue, integration) and offers practices for letting the Ego serve the Self rather than dominate.

/sell.title: To Sell Is Human
/sell.summary: A structured book summary of Daniel Pink's "To Sell Is Human," distilling its framework for modern, win/win selling — including the ABCs of attunement, buoyancy, and clarity, plus pitch techniques, improv principles, and serving others.

/shame.title: Shame
/shame.summary: A psychological framework distinguishing shame ("I am bad, I've been exposed") from guilt ("I did something bad"), arguing that guilt properly applied drives correct behavior while shame drives hiding and rumination. It offers diagnostic "tests" and antidotes (empathy, reframing) to defuse unwarranted self-judgment.

/sharpen-the-saw.title: Sharpen the saw
/sharpen-the-saw.summary: Igor's take on Stephen Covey's Habit 7 ("Sharpen the Saw"), reframed as renewing four dimensions of personal health — physical, emotional/social, mental, and identity/spiritual — to sustain an upward spiral of effectiveness. It argues that this Quadrant II self-renewal is the leveraged, non-urgent work that keeps you sharp for every other habit and lets you script positive potential onto the people around you.
entities: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People

/sicp.title: Structure and Design of Computer Programs
/sicp.summary: Personal study notes on LISP and SICP, covering why Lisp treats code as data (homoiconicity), the lambda-calculus roots of the language, recursion, Church encoding, macros, and tooling for learning Racket in Vim. Also reflects on learning hard technical concepts non-linearly with an interactive tutor.

/side-quests.title: Side Quests: Random Tech Explorations
/side-quests.summary: Igor's running log of "side quests" — small, time-boxed technical explorations done for fun rather than deliverables. Covers a browser-based playing-card detector built with YOLO/ONNX, an iOS app that extracts iPhone health and location data for an AI life coach, and an experiment running macOS VMs on Apple Silicon.

/siy.title: Search inside yourself
/siy.summary: A detailed book summary of Chade-Meng Tan's "Search Inside Yourself" and "Joy on Demand," presenting mindfulness and meditation as trainable practices for building emotional intelligence—covering self-awareness, self-regulation (SBNRR), self-motivation, resilience, and mindful listening, with concrete guided exercises.

/sleep.title: Keep life on easy mode - Prioritize Sleep
/sleep.summary: A personal collection of sleep-optimization tactics and observations, covering tools like the Eight Sleep bed heater/cooler, consistent wake times, caffeine and alcohol effects, the relationship between calorie restriction and reduced sleep need, and the experience of waking up at 5am.

/sleeping-bag-sacrifices.title: Sleeping bag sacrifices
/sleeping-bag-sacrifices.summary: A personal story from Igor's early days on the Windows Azure OS team at Microsoft, where he avoided waking his mentor Jay—who slept under his desk—out of misplaced consideration, only to learn months later that Jay had been staying late specifically to mentor him. The lesson: don't make assumptions about what others want; ask directly rather than deciding for them.

/sleight-of-mouth.title: Sleight Of Mouth
/sleight-of-mouth.summary: A structured set of notes on "Sleight of Mouth" — an NLP-based collection of language and reframing techniques for changing the mental models, beliefs, and meanings that shape our experience. It covers reframing limiting beliefs, chunking, values/criteria, and shifting from problem/failure/critic frames to outcome/feedback/advisor frames.
entities: Logical Levels (Environment, Behavior, Capability, Beliefs, Identity)

/slow.title: Rest and Digest - Sometimes you'd rather go slow
/slow.summary: An argument for intentionally slowing down: the post explains the parasympathetic ("rest and digest") nervous system, why chronic high arousal is dysregulation rather than strength, and offers concrete breathing, physical, and lifestyle techniques to strengthen PNS activation for better sleep, relationships, creativity, and decision-making.

/smilebox.title: Smilebox
/smilebox.summary: Igor revisits a 2012 idea — a "Smilebox" that captures and displays people's smiles — and builds it in 2023 using browser-based face detection. The post embeds the working JavaScript that uses a webcam to detect faces, age, gender, and happiness expressions in real time.

/snjb.title: Welcome to Steve's jungle and bar!
/snjb.summary: A port of Zach's interactive fiction game "Steve's Night Jungle and Bar" (originally built in twee/Twine) to Python running in the browser via Brython. The post embeds the playable game.

/software-leadership-roles.title: Tech leads, software architects and engineering managers - oh my!
/software-leadership-roles.summary: A reference post distilling Kartik Ayya's Quora answer on the four software leadership roles—tech lead, architect, product manager, and engineering manager—and the technical, leadership, and communication skills that differentiate the tech lead from the architect. Igor adds notes on running a team small enough to wear multiple hats and on failure modes when role boundaries blur.

/spiritual-health.title: Spiritual Health: Purpose, Transcendence and Coherence
/spiritual-health.summary: A working-notes essay on "spiritual health," proposing that sustained motivation you'd be proud to model for your child is the real diagnostic for meaning. It breaks meaning into three dimensions (coherence, significance, purpose), surveys frameworks that define it differently, and catalogs common "meaning traps" and spiritual problems with their cures.

/stock-concentration.title: Stock Concentration: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Capital Gains Tax
/stock-concentration.summary: A practical guide for tech employees on the risks of becoming over-concentrated in company stock through RSUs, covering tax-efficient selling strategies, protective options (puts, collars), and exchange funds for tax-deferred diversification.

/stoicism.title: Stoicism
/stoicism.summary: A brief explainer on Stoicism as a secular philosophy for resilience and inner peace, outlining its core practices (locus of control, amor fati, memento mori) drawing on Arthur Brooks and Ryan Holiday's framing.

/strategy.title: On strategy
/strategy.summary: A structured summary of competitive strategy theory — drawing on Michael Porter's "What Is Strategy?" — covering strategic positioning, trade-offs, fit, and sustainable competitive advantage, followed by a section applying strategic thinking to big tech companies (FAANG/the Four, Stratechery's frameworks).

/strengths.title: My Strengths from Strength Finder
/strengths.summary: Igor's personal strengths profile, built from the StrengthsFinder 2.0 assessment (Activator, Communication, Restorative/Resolver, Adaptability, Ideation), with short and long descriptions of each, plus bonus results from his PANAS personality type (Mad Scientist) and Enneagram (top type: The Challenger).

/structure.title: Humane Structure -  A Gentle Framework for Wild Minds
/structure.summary: A manifesto for "humane structure" — habit-tracking and personal systems designed around how humans actually work (forgiving gaps, observing without judging, capturing partial wins) rather than harsh management-style discipline. It breaks tracking into three pillars (awareness, recording, learning) and proposes an LLM-backed, implicitly-captured architecture feeding an AI life coach.
entities: Three pillars of tracking (awareness, recording, learning)

/sublime.title: Training the sublime states
/sublime.summary: A practical guide to the four Buddhist "sublime states" (loving-kindness, compassion, altruistic joy, equanimity) as tools for emotional health, with informal practices, formal meditations, and the "near enemy" failure modes for each state.

/suicide.title: Brain Attacks: A Better Model for Suicide
/suicide.summary: A deeply personal post in which Igor offers advice he wishes he'd received at 20, when his father died by suicide: he reframes suicide as an acute, fatal "brain attack" that is no one's fault, that the disease (not the person) is responsible for the act, and that the hereditary risk should be taken seriously through proactive mental-health care.

/sustainable-work.title: I don't want to die knowing I spent too much time at work, and I bet you don't either
/sustainable-work.summary: An engineering leadership post where Igor describes how he models work-life balance (leaving at 4pm, no evening/weekend email, taking vacations) and the team mechanisms he uses to help reports avoid burnout, illustrated by a new mother's story of reducing her load before transitioning to part-time.

/switch.title: Switch
/switch.summary: Book notes on "Switch" by Chip and Dan Heath, summarizing its three-part framework for driving behavioral change (direct the Rider, motivate the Elephant, shape the Path) and mapping it to other change-management models like Influencer 2.0 and classical Greek rhetoric.

/synergize.title: Creating Synergy: Making 1+1 = 3!
/synergize.summary: Igor's personal notes on Habit 6 (Synergize) from Stephen Covey's 7 Habits, exploring how real differences, trust, and intent produce creative "1+1=3" outcomes through third-alternative thinking — illustrated with examples from engineering management, marriage, and meetings.
entities: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People

/system-design.title: System Design Questions
/system-design.summary: A personal reference index and study checklist for system design interview problems, covering the standard framework (users, APIs, data, scaling, capacity estimation) plus specific techniques like geo-indexing, consistent hashing, and worked use cases such as URL shorteners, chat systems, and nearby-friends services.

/taas.title: Transportation as a Service
/taas.summary: A summary of John Zimmer's (Lyft co-founder) essay arguing that Transportation as a Service — ride sharing plus autonomous vehicle fleets — is the third transportation revolution that will end private car ownership in cities and let urban space be redesigned for people rather than cars.

/taxes.title: Taxes — Capital Gains, WA State, Step-Up, and QSBS
/taxes.summary: An engineer-written tax reference covering capital gains (short vs. long-term), Washington State capital-gains mechanics, step-up in basis at death, and retirement-account mechanics (IRAs, 401(k)s, Roth, RMDs), with worked examples of effective vs. marginal rates and retirement-realization timing strategies. Aimed at high-earning software engineers planning around tax optimization.

/td/alexa-skill.title: Make an Alexa Skill
/td/alexa-skill.summary: A short technical diary entry documenting the author's attempt to build a "hello world" Amazon Alexa skill using the Serverless framework, including notes on downgrading the Serverless CLI version to fix broken Alexa auth and deploying Python and C# skills.

/td/back_ref.title: Adding back references to my blog
/td/back_ref.summary: A short technical design note outlining Igor's strategy for adding back-links to his densely forward-linked Jekyll blog, covering when to compute them, the search-engine-style methodology for building a page-to-references map, and how to inject them into source content cleanly.

/td/better-security-design.title: Security Notes
/td/better-security-design.summary: A brain-dump of security design musings from the author's prior career in security, cataloging good and bad authentication patterns, threat-modeling frameworks, and assorted security topics. It highlights what works (SSO, Microsoft Authenticator's number-matching flow, password managers, MFA) versus what's weak (email/SMS password resets) and outlines security-assessment methods like STRIDE and threat modeling.

/td/cloud-first-applications.title: Cloud First Applications
/td/cloud-first-applications.summary: Igor's working notes on cloud-first application architecture, covering containers, microservices, container orchestration, API gateways, networking patterns (REST vs WebSockets), service meshes, serverless functions, and distributed-system resilience patterns.

/td/data-systems.title: Data Systems
/td/data-systems.summary: A technical reference page summarizing data systems concepts, largely distilled from "Designing Data-Intensive Applications," covering analytics vs OLTP, data lakes vs warehouses, stream vs batch processing, and a catalog of AWS database and message-broker services.

/td/dump_imessage_history.title: Accessing iMessage history
/td/dump_imessage_history.summary: A technical how-to for extracting and querying your full iMessage history on macOS by accessing the chat.db SQLite database directly, including the Full Disk Access setup, iCloud sync prerequisites, and reverse-engineered SQL queries (with date conversion) to dump all messages.

/td/fix_ssh.title: SSH Client Commands Timing Out from Some Clients Sometimes
/td/fix_ssh.summary: A technical troubleshooting note on diagnosing a hanging SSH connection by running sshd in debug mode on the server and verbose ssh on the client, then comparing logs to discover the root cause was an MTU issue.

/td/hack-web.title: Hacking the web for fun and profit
/td/hack-web.summary:
A technical how-to collection of browser-based web hacking tricks: injecting JavaScript via bookmarklets, userscripts, and the DevTools console to scrape, automate, and reverse-engineer websites. Includes real examples like extracting Alexa voice recordings, ripping Kindle Cloud Reader books, and debugging obfuscated anti-debugging JS.</parameter>
</invoke>

/td/ios.title: iOS and iPadOS tips
/td/ios.summary: A personal reference list of iOS and iPadOS tips, gestures, and recommended apps that Igor uses heavily on his iPhone and iPad — covering global controls, health/sleep tracking, photography, AirPods customization, handwriting/note-taking apps, Apple Watch faces, AR apps, and keyboard shortcuts.

/td/ios-nomad.title: iOS Software Engineer Nomad How-To
/td/ios-nomad.summary: A practical guide to doing all your software development from an iPad ("iPad developer nomad"), covering three workflows—remote (SSH to a cloud box), local (Linux emulation and native iOS apps), and hybrid (git-synced between the two)—plus external keyboard, stand, and key-remapping tips.

/td/linqpad_from_redshift.title: Make LINQPad Work with Redshift
/td/linqpad_from_redshift.summary: A technical how-to diary on getting LINQPad to work against Amazon Redshift by rebuilding the Postgres LINQPad driver (Npgsql) to support Redshift, plus debugging notes for connecting via psql, reverse-engineering schemas, and writing time-bucketed Redshift SQL.

/td/minecraft.title: Minecraft Notes
/td/minecraft.summary: A personal tech-diary of notes on running and modding Minecraft (Bedrock edition) for kids, covering dedicated server hosting on AWS Lightsail/Docker, the Xbox UDP proxy hack for LAN servers, world editing, and scripting via command blocks, functions, and resource/behavior packs.

/td/private_web_site.title: Private Web Site
/td/private_web_site.summary: A short tech-diary note planning how to host a private version of the author's Jekyll blog so unfinished ideas stay restricted to a chosen set of people, using Azure's one-step Active Directory authentication to secure the deployed static site.

/td/ring-video-download.title: Ring Video Doorbell
/td/ring-video-download.summary: A short tech-diary post documenting a personal project to download and archive all Ring video doorbell footage via Python, sync it to cloud storage (OneDrive), and avoid duplicate downloads — including notes on an API limitation capping downloads at the last 100 events.

/td/slow_zsh.title: Slow ZSH prompts
/td/slow_zsh.summary: A debugging walkthrough of why pressing enter in zsh suddenly took seconds: the author traces it to shell-history search tools (mcfly, then Atuin) performing slow SQLite disk writes that only stall when running inside tmux, using zprof and a custom I/O profiler to isolate the bottleneck.

/td/sqlalchemy-redshift.title: SQLAlchemy with Redshift
/td/sqlalchemy-redshift.summary: A technical how-to from Igor's tech diary showing how to connect to Amazon Redshift from Python using SQLAlchemy: installing dependencies, configuring SSL certificates, building the engine connection string, reflecting database tables/schemas, and querying via ORM sessions and automapped classes.

/td/stats.title: Statistics
/td/stats.summary: A study-notes reference post outlining core statistics concepts — inferential vs descriptive statistics, p-values and confidence intervals, error types, precision/recall, multi-armed bandits, and ways to summarize data sets.

/td/streaks.title: Habit tracking app
/td/streaks.summary: A tech-diary note about building a daily-habit streak-tracking app as a learning project for Firebase and React, listing functional requirements (auth, offline storage, responsive UI, multi-streak tracking, reminders, stats), later annotated that the existing Streaks app already did this perfectly and could serve as a template project for learning new languages/libraries.

/td/usbtech.title: USB Tech
/td/usbtech.summary: A personal reference brain-dump on USB charging technology, covering the wattage limits and quirks of legacy USB 2.0 charging versus the faster USB Power Delivery (PD) standard, with practical buying recommendations for chargers, cables, and Apple device fast-charging.

/td/virtual-desktops.title: Virtual desktops in Wox
/td/virtual-desktops.summary: A technical diary entry documenting how Igor built a Wox launcher plugin to switch Windows 10 virtual desktops, using Python ctypes P/Invoke to call a C++ bridge DLL (VirtualDesktopAccessor) that wraps the undocumented COM virtual desktop APIs.

/td/visual-vocabulary.title: Visual Thinking Notes
/td/visual-vocabulary.summary: A personal notes post cataloging resources, tools, and references that help Igor with visual thinking—drawing, sketching, building a visual vocabulary, SVG editing, and creating pencasts/whiteboard videos.

/td/windbg.title: WinDbg Notes
/td/windbg.summary: A short technical diary entry collecting WinDBG debugging commands, focused on debugging .NET/CLR processes: setting breakpoints when the runtime loads, loading the SOS extension, and dumping the managed call stack.

/tech-health-toys.title: Tech for health
/tech-health-toys.summary: A personal roundup of the health and fitness tracking gadgets, apps, and wearables the author uses (and a few that didn't work) to measure and motivate their physical health, including heart rate, sleep, weight, and blood glucose monitoring.

/tech-rituals.title: Tech Rituals
/tech-rituals.summary: A personal checklist-style post for scheduling periodic (monthly/quarterly) reviews of one's developer tooling — AI coding tools, Jupyter health-tracking notebooks, and linters/formatters/pre-commit hooks — so you stay current as tech changes fast.

/terzepatide.title: Terzepatide: The magic weight loss shot
/terzepatide.summary: Igor's personal account of using tirzepatide (a GLP-1 weight-loss drug) starting February 2024 — how it suppresses appetite by slowing digestion, how dieting strategy must change versus calorie restriction, the side effects, and how he sources it via compounding pharmacy.

/tesla.title: Tony (Tessie) the Tesla
/tesla.summary: Igor's personal review of owning a Tesla Model Y, centered on its Autopilot self-driving for someone who dislikes driving, plus practical notes on charging/range trade-offs, sleeping/camping in the car, and turning charging downtime into productive time. Also introduces Tony, his Tesla-named AI life coach.

/test-auto-sunburst.title: Test Auto-Generated Sunburst
/test-auto-sunburst.summary: A test/demo page that exercises the blog's auto-generated sunburst chart feature, which builds a hierarchical tree visualization from the page's H2 and H3 headings. The body is placeholder content (Category A/B with subcategories and items) used to verify the rendering.

/testing.title: Testing and Quality
/testing.summary: A reference catalog of software testing types and practices — from unit, integration, and end-to-end tests to non-functional testing (load, stress, security, fuzz) and testing in production — arguing that untested code doesn't work and that testability drives better software design.

/the-recruiter-does-not-think-you-are-ho.title: The recruiter does not think you're hot
/the-recruiter-does-not-think-you-are-ho.summary: A satirical first-person anecdote about taking a bad software engineering job because the recruiter was attractive, then regretting it during a death-march project. The punchline-driven moral: don't choose a job based on the recruiter rather than the work itself.

/time-traveler.title: When Past Igor Meets Future Igor
/time-traveler.summary: A reflective framework post on "mental time travel" — using imagined conversations between past, present, and future selves to make better life decisions, balance present enjoyment against future benefit, and avoid common regrets. It covers delayed gratification, work/life balance over time, and wasteful uses of time.

/timeoff.title: Getting the most out of time off
/timeoff.summary: A personal playbook treating time off as a learnable skill: it maps the phases of a vacation (vegetation, todo-list, freedom, productivity), warns against ignoring energizing habits during downtime, and compiles Igor's detailed checklists for managing family expectations, physical/emotional/identity health, diet, and travel logistics.
entities: Four Sublime States (Loving Kindness, Compassion, Altruistic Joy, Equanimity)
Begin with the End in Mind

/timeoff-2020-03.title: Adventures March and April 2020
/timeoff-2020-03.summary: A planning document for Igor's 2-month sabbatical in March 2020 (between Amazon and Facebook), listing goals and progress across fitness, blogging, magic, biking, family time, and side projects, plus notes from books he read. The COVID-19 outbreak coincided with the time off and disrupted his focus.

The Five Dysfunctions of a Team

/timeoff-2020-12.title: Time off X-mas 2020
/timeoff-2020-12.summary: A personal planning document for Igor's two-week holiday break during the 2020 COVID-19 Christmas, laying out goals and checklists across life domains—relationships, identity/magic, physical, emotional, cognitive, and mental health—to maximize personal development and stay balanced rather than vegetate.

/timeoff-2021-11.title: Time off Turkey Day 2021
/timeoff-2021-11.summary: A personal retrospective on a November 2021 vacation, reviewing how Igor spent his time off across family, tech projects, and health habits. The core lesson: pre-plan vacations to stay deliberate and balanced rather than getting absorbed into one area such as tech at the expense of habits like magic, meditation, and exercise.

/timeoff-2021-12.title: Time off X-mas 2021
/timeoff-2021-12.summary: A personal time-off planning and retrospective post for Igor's two-week 2021 winter holiday, where he pre-writes and then reviews goals across life domains (family, health, habits, work) with an emphasis on balance and minimizing "vegetation."

/timeoff-2022-02.title: Time off Mid winter break 2022
/timeoff-2022-02.summary: Igor's pre-written and retrospective plan for a one-week winter staycation, organized as a balance-across-life-domains checklist (relationships, magic, biking, blogging, meditation, health) aimed at maximizing personal development and avoiding vegetation during time off.

/timeoff-2022-07.title: Time off July 2022 - FISM and Canada.
/timeoff-2022-07.summary: A personal time-off planning and reflection journal for Igor's July 2022 trip to Quebec City for FISM (the "olympics of magic"), structured around his evergreen happiness/priorities framework and combining vacation, family time, and magic. It logs intentions and post-trip moments across health habits, family, magic performances, emotional health, and lessons learned.

/timeoff-2022-11.title: Time off November 2022 - New York with Ammon, and Then Maureen and Brian for Thanksgiving
/timeoff-2022-11.summary: A personal time-off journal entry covering Igor's pre-Thanksgiving 2022 trip to NYC (Broadway musicals, magic, comedy) with his friend Ammon, framed around his priority and habit checklist for vacationing well while juggling work layoffs and a reorg remotely. It blends trip moments with his evergreen system for happiness, healthy habits, and keeping work boundaries.
entities: Harry Potter and the Cursed Child

/timeoff-2022-12.title: Time off X-mas 2022
/timeoff-2022-12.summary: Igor's year-end (Christmas 2022) time-off retrospective and planning post, scoring himself across personal-development dimensions—health, relationships, identity, and "tech guru" tinkering—and noting that his biggest learning is to deliberately plan family "moments" rather than rely on good intentions. A large chunk celebrates his early experiments using GPT/AI for coding, git commit messages, and blog image generation.

/timeoff-2023-04.title: Time off April Break 2023
/timeoff-2023-04.summary: Igor's spring break 2023 time-off planning and retrospective post, structured around his evergreen life-area goals (health, relationships, identity, emotional and inner-peace health) during a family trip to Canada to visit relatives.

/timeoff-2023-08.title: Time off August 2023
/timeoff-2023-08.summary: Igor's pre-written plan for an August 2023 family summer-break vacation to the coast, structured as a self-tracking checklist across his evergreen life dimensions (health, relationships, identity, emotional and physical habits) with daily goals and reflections on what went well.

/timeoff-2023-11.title: Ashland T-Day 2023
/timeoff-2023-11.summary: A pre-written planning and review template for Igor's annual Thanksgiving 2023 family trip to Oregon, scoring intentions across life dimensions like health, relationships, meditation, creative output, and inner peace.

/timeoff-2024-02.title: Time off Midwinter break 2024 - Divide and conquer
/timeoff-2024-02.summary: A personal life-journal entry reflecting on Igor's midwinter week off, where he used a "divide and conquer" approach to spend one-on-one mini-vacations with each family member while restarting hobbies like magic, balloons, and kettlebells. He reviews his recharge across identity, cognitive, physical, and relational health, and reflects on his own discipline and mental blocks.

/timeoff-2024-04.title: Time off Spring break 2024 - Chillin
/timeoff-2024-04.summary: A spring break 2024 personal journal entry where Igor reflects on a week off, tracking time with family, physical and mental health, identity practices (magic, biking), and a set of tech and blogging side-projects and learnings.

/timeoff-2024-08.title: Time off August 2024
/timeoff-2024-08.summary: A personal planning document for Igor's 6-week summer 2024 time off, mapping out week-by-week itineraries split between each kid, solo time, and family travel, alongside goals for health, habits, blogging, AI side projects, and emotional wellbeing.

/timeoff-2025-08.title: Time off August 2025 - Meta Recharge
/timeoff-2025-08.summary: A pre-written and progressively-updated planning journal for Igor's 6-week summer 2025 sabbatical from Meta, organized by week (road trips to Crater Lake, Long Beach, Yellowstone/Grand Teton) and by life-area goals spanning family relationships, health habits, content creation, and AI/tech projects.

/timeoff-2026-07.title: Time off July 2026 - Scandinavian Whirlwind Tour
/timeoff-2026-07.summary: A pre-trip itinerary for a 22-day family vacation through 5 European countries (Iceland, Denmark, Sweden, Norway, Netherlands), laying out per-leg plans and personal goals like quality time with the kids and disconnecting from work.

/todo_enjoy.title: Things I enjoy
/todo_enjoy.summary: A personal reminder list of the many small activities, hobbies, and rituals that make Igor happy — spanning errands, health practices, family time, performance/clowning, magic, and juggling — framed within his "begin with the end in mind" eulogy philosophy.
entities: Begin with the end in mind

/tools.title: Igor's Tech, Tricks, and Tools
/tools.summary: A personal, ever-growing reference page cataloging the command-line tools, developer utilities, and one-liner recipes Igor uses across Unix/CLI, Python, git, web tooling, video editing, diagramming, and more. It's a tips-and-tricks cheat sheet rather than a narrative essay.

/tori.title: Tori
/tori.summary: A brief, deliberately Tori-light page in which Igor reflects on becoming and being a husband, focusing on his own growth as a partner while honoring his wife Tori's privacy.

/touching.title: Things that make me cry
/touching.summary: A curated collection of touching, heartwarming media — commercials, speeches, short films, movies, and audio stories — plus personal reflections, gathered as an antidote to cynicism and a reminder of the good in humanity.

/toysfortots.title: Donating to charity
/toysfortots.summary: A retrospective on one of Igor's most satisfying career projects at Amazon: building the feature that let Alexa customers donate to charity (including Toys for Tots) with Amazon matching their donations, going from zero to an MVP announced live on Good Morning America in under four months. It recounts the launch-day war room, debugging intermittent purchase failures caused by dynamic inventory pricing, and credits the team that shipped it.

/tribe.title: My Tribe
/tribe.summary: A curated list of writers, thinkers, and "learning in public" practitioners Igor admires and follows as role models, organized into personal "tribes," plus practical notes like getting RSS feeds from Substack/Medium.

/untangled.title: Untangled: Guiding Teenage Girls Through the Seven Transitions into Adulthood
/untangled.summary: Igor's distilled notes from Lisa Damour's book "Untangled," reframing the developmental "strands" of adolescent girls (separating from childhood, joining a new peer tribe, puberty, social drama) as normal and offering concrete strategies for parents to stay connected without taking the rejection personally.

/upstream.title: Upstream - How to solve problems before they happen
/upstream.summary: A summary of Dan Heath's book "Upstream," which argues for solving problems before they start (proactive prevention vs. reactive response). It covers what blocks people from operating upstream (problem blindness, lack of ownership, tunneling/lack of slack) and key questions for upstream interventions (uniting allies, changing systems, finding leverage, early warning, avoiding harm, who pays).
entities: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People

/vibing.title: The Psychology of Vibing
/vibing.summary: A survey of "vibing" — extended conversational work alongside AI agents for coding, writing, and thinking — naming its benefits (throughput, flow, always-on availability) alongside its dark sides: addiction-like compulsion, loss of self-awareness in perpetual flow, and burnout measured in lost sleep and dropping HRV.

/vim.title: Igors Vim Tips
/vim.summary: A personal cheat sheet of Vim and Neovim tips, keybindings, and plugin workflows the author wants to practice and remember, covering code editing, LSP-based navigation, and Git integration.

/vim-for-writing.title: Vim For Writing
/vim-for-writing.summary: A how-to reference for turning Vim into a comfortable free-form prose and Markdown writing environment, covering soft line wrapping, distraction-free display, paragraph focus, spell-check, thesaurus, linting, and table-of-contents tooling via specific plugins.

/viz.title: Data Visualizations and Visual Storytelling
/viz.summary: A concise guide to effective data visualization: how to choose the right chart type for your message, use diagrams as a visual vocabulary, and craft visualizations that tell stories leading to action rather than just displaying raw data.

/vlc.title: IINA Player Keyboard Shortcuts
/vlc.summary: A keyboard-shortcut reference guide for the IINA media player (migrated from VLC), covering playback, speed, chapters, subtitles, audio/volume, and window controls, with a short section on mpv CLI keybindings.

/voices.title: Voices in my head - A survey of my personalities
/voices.summary: Igor describes a self-management technique, borrowed from negotiator William Ury, of naming the independent "voices" in his subconscious (positive players, negative habits, anxieties) and talking to each one with compassion to understand their interests. He shares his own personal roster of named voices and a diagram of the willpower-vs-impulse dynamic.

/walking-with-god.title: Walking with God: A Daily Devotional Journey
/walking-with-god.summary: Igor, a non-religious engineer, reads a daily Christian devotional alongside someone important to him and documents his practice of translating its religious wisdom into secular, engineering-friendly frameworks. The post collects dated entries pairing scripture with his own equivalents drawn from personal affirmations, Stoicism, essentialism, and self-compassion.
entities: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
Circle of Influence / Circle of Concern

/wally.title: Wally and My Work Gastown
/wally.summary: Igor reframes Steve Yegge's "Gas Town" multi-agent pattern into FAANG org-chart vocabulary, describing how he runs his AI coding agent "Wally" as an M1 manager with cloud-based staff and ephemeral specialized workers ("Odallies"), with himself as the M2 manager-of-managers. He argues for a missing third tier and shares lessons on training agents, naming ephemeral workers, and operating a probabilistic agent stack.

/warm.title: Staying warm
/warm.summary: A practical gear roundup of products Igor uses to stay warm in cold, wet weather — both in everyday life and on long winter bike rides — including heated apparel, gloves, compression layers, and waterproof cycling kit.

/weeks.title: Life in Weeks
/weeks.summary: A "Life in Weeks" visualization where every week the author has been alive is rendered as a small box, annotated with meaningful events (good and bad) to reflect on how much of life is shaped by these moments. It's inspired by Tim Urban's Wait But Why concept.

/welcome-to-holland.title: Welcome to Holland
/welcome-to-holland.summary:
A reprint of Emily Perl Kingsley's 1987 essay "Welcome to Holland," which uses a travel metaphor (planning a trip to Italy but landing in Holland) to describe raising a child with a disability and the lifelong work of grieving the expected life while accepting and finding beauty in the actual one.</parameter>
</invoke>

/what-makes-a-leader.title: What makes a leader
/what-makes-a-leader.summary: A summary of Dan Goleman's "What Makes a Leader," arguing that emotional intelligence is the crux of leadership and breaking it into five components—self-awareness, self-regulation, motivation, empathy, and social skill—each with its defining hallmarks.

/why-is-no-one-referring.title: Why Is No One Referring My Project
/why-is-no-one-referring.summary: A short business post arguing that customers withhold referrals not from laziness or lack of a ready-made message, but because making a referral puts their own credibility at risk. It frames three barriers to referrals (work, message, credibility risk) and says to tackle them in reverse order, prioritizing credibility risk first.

/win-win.title: Win/win or no deal
/win-win.summary: A personal distillation of Stephen Covey's "Think Win/Win" habit (7 Habits, Chapter 4), covering the six paradigms of human interaction, the Emotional Bank Account model of trust, and the concrete deposits that build durable relationships in interdependent settings.
entities: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People

/words-we-dont-have.title: The Words We Don't Have
/words-we-dont-have.summary: An exploration of how language shapes thought, walking through the Sapir-Whorf hypothesis (linguistic relativity) with examples like the four Greek words for love, Inuit snow vocabulary, and color/spatial perception, plus Orwell's Newspeak as deliberate manipulation. Igor argues that expanding your vocabulary expands the experiences and distinctions you can have.

/work-satisfaction.title: Satisfaction is not the lack of dissatisfaction
/work-satisfaction.summary: Igor's framework for thinking about work satisfaction (SAT) and dissatisfaction (DSAT) as two orthogonal dimensions, presented as a 2x2 matrix with diagnostic signals and strategies for raising SAT, lowering DSAT, and deciding when to change teams or companies.

/write-book.title: How to write a book
/write-book.summary: Igor's working notes on writing nonfiction books, drawn mainly from the book "Write Useful Books," plus a transcript of a brainstorming session with a GPT persona of his best friend to title his engineering-manager book ("How to EM").

/writing.title: Writing is Thinking
/writing.summary: A guide to writing as critical thinking.

/y24.title: Igor's plan for 2024
/y24.summary: Igor's "begin with the end in mind" 2024 year-in-review and goal-setting post, drafted at the start of the year and annotated with end-of-year updates covering major projects, physical and emotional health goals, hobbies, reading, and things he built.
entities: What I wish I knew at 42

/y25.title: Igor's plan for 2025
/y25.summary: A forward-written annual review for 2025, framing the year's goals as if already accomplished: major projects (content creation, AI tools, EM handbook), tracked physical/emotional/identity health metrics, family relationships, and a tally of blog/code artifacts produced.

/y26.title: Igor's plan for 2026
/y26.summary: Igor's begin-with-the-end-in-mind annual plan for 2026, written as a pre-dated year-end report covering major projects (AI tools, content creation, Time.ltd/Larry life coach), physical/emotional/spiritual/identity health goals tracked in metric tables, family rituals, and a weekly/monthly/quarterly review cadence framed around fighting the three dragons of entropy, squander, and scarcity.

/shoulder-pain.title: Shoulder Pain: The Indirect Approach
/shoulder-pain.summary: Igor's account of developing shoulder impingement from Turkish Get-Ups done without the mobility or form to do them safely, and the indirect fix: restore thoracic-spine mobility, loosen pecs and lats, then rebuild rotator-cuff strength — treating the shoulder as a symptom of upstream problems.
